page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is a compute-focused server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 112 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 56 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled. The memory configuration provides 448 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. The processor includes an extensive cache hierarchy, consisting of a 1792 KB L1 data cache, 28 MB L2 cache, and 448 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Memory subsystem performance is also highly competitive, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching the top 10% at 7743.53 GB/sec, while large-block read and write bandwidths fall within the top 25%. In computational tasks, the instance achieves top 10% performance in ray tracing and physics simulations, alongside strong top 25% results in software compilation, database operations, and static web serving throughput. However, single-threaded operations and large-model LLM text generation represent relative performance bottlenecks, with 70B model text generation falling into the bottom 25%.

Given its high core density and dedicated CPU allocation, the c2d-standard-112 offers strong resource density for parallelized workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded compute tasks, software compilation pipelines, physics engines, and high-throughput web serving. It also serves as an efficient platform for medium-scale database operations and Redis deployments, though workloads requiring heavy single-threaded execution or large-scale LLM inference may find less optimal resource utilization.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is a compute-focused instance featuring 112 dedicated vCPUs and 448 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 56 physical cores and a 448 MB L3 cache, this server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, particularly for cached small-block reads. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, making it a pure CPU-compute resource. Benchmarks highlight strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, physics simulations, and static web serving. However, single-threaded tasks and large-model LLM text generation show moderate to weak results. The instance is highly suited for highly parallelized workloads, including multi-threaded compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving, offering a balanced resource profile for compute-heavy enterprise applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is a compute-focused server featuring 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 448 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance optimized for parallel enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is a compute-focused server with 112 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 448 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core-density x86_64 compute platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and balanced memory for highly parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 112 dedicated vCPUs and 448 GB RAM powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high cached memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators, optimizing cost for pure CPU workloads
Strong performance in software compilation, physics simulations, and database operations
Best suited for highly parallelized tasks, web serving, and multi-threaded applications
